key_insight: Intrinsic ductility can be predicted by the energy needed to amorphize under shear, rather than by classical crack-cleavage or dislocation-based criteria.
engineering_relevance: This offers a fast design rule for screening pure metals and multi-principal-element alloys for room-temperature ductility and ductile-to-brittle transition temperature.
method: Derive structure-property relations that compress electronic structure, band filling, and shear effects into a single ductility framework.
Use analytical expressions with ab initio stiffness constants, lattice parameters, and binary interaction energies.
Apply the model to phase diagrams and alloy design, including the Nb-Ta-V-Ti system.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports agreement with predictions of intrinsic ductility and ductile-to-brittle transition temperatures, and shows phase diagrams for Nb-Ta-V-Ti that explain its strength and tensile ductility.
watch_item: The framework depends on available ab initio inputs and tabulated materials data, so transferability to systems with sparse or uncertain property data may require caution.
paper_title: Intrinsic Ductility from Shear Amorphization: From Pure Metals to Multi-Principal-Element Alloys
